meta.title: VantageStarter — AI-Native SaaS Boilerplate
meta.description: The only boilerplate where AI renders components, not text. Ship faster with Generative UI, Convex real-time, credit system, and a 36-agent army built in.
meta.og_title: VantageStarter — Your AI builds the UI.
meta.og_description: Ship AI SaaS products without rebuilding the plumbing. Generative UI, Convex real-time, credits, auth, billing — all included.

hero.subline: Every SaaS starter kit bolted AI on later. VantageStarter was architected for agents from commit one — real-time backend, generative UI, credit billing, 36-agent plugin army included.
hero.social_proof_micro: Built on Convex. Shipped with Clerk. Powered by AI SDK v6.

features.heading: The stack that assumes AI is the product
features.subheading: Everything you need to ship your SaaS fast — auth, billing, real-time backend, and AI, all pre-wired.
features.ai_renders_desc: AI SDK v6 + Generative UI. Your agents return interactive React components — not walls of markdown the user has to parse.
features.realtime_desc: Convex reactive queries. Zero config. Your data updates everywhere, the moment it changes. No polling. No websocket boilerplate.
features.credits_desc: AI costs money. The credit system is in the schema, not an afterthought. Metered usage, balance tracking, and gate logic — already wired.
features.media_title: Media generation out of the box
features.media_desc: fal.ai for images and video. Firecrawl for web intelligence. Connected, typed, ready. Not a "coming soon" integration page.
features.agents_desc: A Claude Code plugin army that writes, audits, deploys, scores, and ships. Vibe coders get a team, not a folder of components.
features.i18n_desc: i18n is infrastructure, not a feature request six months in. Seven languages. Every string. Day zero.

pricing.heading: One product. Two options. No per-seat nonsense.
pricing.one_time_note: One-time payment. Build unlimited projects. No subscriptions. No renewals. Yours forever.
pricing.starter_desc: The architecture, open. See what agentic-native looks like before you commit.
pricing.pro_desc: Everything. No upsells. No team tier. No per-seat pricing. Your entire stack.

faq.heading: Questions
faq.q1_a: A complete Next.js 15 codebase with Convex backend (13 tables), Clerk auth (SSO, MFA, orgs), Polar.sh billing (subscriptions + one-time), AI SDK v6 with Generative UI, a credit system, fal.ai integration, Firecrawl integration, i18n in 7 languages, 7 OKLCH color presets, Chat UI with tool-call rendering, and a 36-agent Claude Code plugin that helps you build on the codebase. Plus docs, a Discord community, and lifetime updates.
faq.q2_q: What is Generative UI? Why does it matter?
faq.q2_a: Most boilerplates give you a chat box that returns text. Generative UI means your AI returns actual React components — charts, forms, cards, tables — rendered in real-time. The user interacts with UI, not markdown. This is how ChatGPT, v0, and Claude Artifacts work. VantageStarter gives you this pattern out of the box with json-render for structured output.
faq.q3_a: Every AI call costs money. The credit system tracks usage per user, enforces limits, and integrates with billing. It is built into the Convex schema — not a wrapper you add later. When a user runs an AI action, credits debit automatically. When they run out, gates block further usage until they upgrade. This is table-stakes for any AI product, and no other boilerplate ships it.
faq.q4_a: A Claude Code plugin with 36 specialized agents: frontend dev, Convex expert, Clerk expert, security auditor, SEO specialist, product manager, and more. When you work on VantageStarter, these agents understand the codebase, follow its conventions, and build features correctly. It is not AI-assisted coding — it is a dev team in your terminal.
faq.q5_q: What tech stack is this built on?
faq.q5_a: Frontend: Next.js 15, React 19, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui. Backend: Convex (real-time database + server functions). Auth: Clerk (SSO, MFA, organizations, webhooks). Billing: Polar.sh (subscriptions, one-time, webhooks). AI: Vercel AI SDK v6, json-render. Media: fal.ai (image/video generation). Scraping: Firecrawl. i18n: next-intl (7 locales). Styling: OKLCH color system, 7 presets.
faq.q6_q: Why Convex instead of Supabase or Prisma?
faq.q6_a: Convex is a reactive database. When data changes, every connected client updates instantly — no websockets to configure, no polling, no cache invalidation. For AI products where agents modify data in real-time, this is not optional. Supabase requires RLS rules and manual real-time setup. Prisma has no real-time story. Convex makes real-time the default.
faq.q7_a: Polar.sh is built for developers and digital products. Simpler API, native support for license keys, usage-based billing, and GitHub integration. Stripe is powerful but complex — its webhook handling alone takes days to get right. Polar.sh gives you checkout, subscriptions, and customer portal with less code and less surface area for bugs.
faq.q8_q: Why Clerk instead of NextAuth or Supabase Auth?
faq.q8_a: Clerk is a complete identity platform: social SSO, MFA, organizations, RBAC, user management dashboard, webhook sync to your database. NextAuth requires building all of this yourself. Supabase Auth is good but tied to the Supabase ecosystem. Clerk works with any backend and provides the admin tools you will need at scale.
faq.q9_q: Can I use this with Cursor, Copilot, or other AI editors?
faq.q9_a: Yes. The 36-agent plugin is designed for Claude Code, but the codebase itself works with any AI editor. It is clean TypeScript with strict settings, consistent patterns, and extensive type safety — exactly what AI editors need to generate correct code.
faq.q10_q: Do I need to know Convex?
faq.q10_a: No. The 36-agent plugin includes a Convex expert that writes queries, mutations, and actions for you. The schema is documented. The patterns are consistent. If you can read TypeScript, you can work with Convex in an afternoon.
faq.q11_q: Is this just for AI apps?
faq.q11_a: No. The auth, billing, i18n, and UI layers work for any SaaS. The AI features (Generative UI, credits, fal.ai, Firecrawl) are there if you need them — and most apps will, sooner than you think.
faq.q12_q: How is this different from ShipFast?
faq.q12_a: ShipFast was built before the agentic era. It has no real-time database, no credit system, no AI layer, and no agent support. VantageStarter was architected for AI products from commit one. See the full comparison at /compare/shipfast.
faq.q13_q: How is this different from MakerKit?
faq.q13_a: MakerKit's AI story is developer experience — its MCP server helps your editor write code. VantageStarter's AI features ship to your users: generative interfaces, credit billing, media generation, web intelligence. See the full comparison at /compare/makerkit.
faq.q14_a: If you purchase the Pro tier and find VantageStarter does not meet your needs within 30 days, contact us for a full refund. No questions asked.
faq.q15_a: We ship updates continuously. The codebase tracks the latest stable versions of Next.js, Convex, Clerk, and AI SDK. You get lifetime updates — when we improve it for our own projects, you get the same update.

cta.heading: Your AI-native SaaS. Ready in minutes.
cta.subline: Stop rebuilding the plumbing. VantageStarter gives you the full stack — real-time backend, auth, billing, generative UI, and 36 agents — so you can ship what matters.

accessibility_plan.title: Accessibility Improvement Plan
accessibility_plan.description: This plan describes the actions planned to improve the accessibility of VantageStarter in line with RGAA 4.1.2.
accessibility_plan.action1: Complete ARIA labelling on all interactive components
accessibility_plan.action2: Improve keyboard navigation in dashboard views
accessibility_plan.action3: Audit and fix colour contrast ratios (WCAG 1.4.3)
accessibility_plan.action4: Add skip-to-content link on all public pages
